revision.comment: 農林技師・全国区選出参議院議員
revision.text:
'''宮本 邦彦'''（みやもと くにひこ、[[1899年]]（[[明治]]32年）9月6日<ref name=shintei>『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』605頁。</ref> - [[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年{{R|shintei}}<ref group="注">『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』421頁では昭和58年。</ref>）4月24日{{R|shintei}}）は、昭和期の[[農林水産省|農林]][[技官]]、[[実業家]]、[[政治家]]。[[参議院|参議院議員]]。
==経歴== [[長野県]]<ref name=giki>『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』421頁。</ref>{{R|giki}}[[長野市]]出身{{R|shintei}}。1928年（昭和3年）[[九州大学大学院農学研究院・大学院生物資源環境科学府・農学部|九州帝国大学農学部]]農学科を卒業した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
[[高知県庁|高知県]]農林技師、[[宮城県庁|宮城県]]農林技師、[[興亜院]]技師などを務めた{{R|giki}}。戦後[[農林水産省|農林省]]に入省し{{R|shintei}}、農林技師、関東開拓事務所長、農林省東京農地事務局事業部長、同建設部長などを務めた{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
1950年（昭和25年）6月の[[第2回参議院議員通常選挙]]で[[全国区制|全国区]]に[[自由党 (日本 1950-1955)|自由党]]公認で出馬して当選し<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』540頁。</ref>、参議院議員に1期在任した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。1956年（昭和31年）7月の[[第4回参議院議員通常選挙|第4回通常選挙]]で全国区に[[自由民主党 (日本)|自由民主党]]公認で立候補したが落選した<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』547頁。</ref>。
その他、全国土地改良協会理事、農業土木学会（現[[農業農村工学会]]）理事、宮本地質コンサルタント社長などを務めた{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
==脚注== === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *衆議院・参議院『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』大蔵省印刷局、1990年。 *『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』日外アソシエーツ、2003年。 *『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』日外アソシエーツ、2017年。
{{デフォルトソート:みやもと くにひこ}} [[Category:農林技官]] [[Category:日本の実業家]] [[Category:全国区選出の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:九州大学出身の人物]] [[Category:長野県出身の人物]] [[Category:1899年生]] [[Category:1984年没]]

==経歴== [[長野県]]<ref name=giki>『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』421頁。</ref>[[長野市]]出身{{R|shintei}}。1928年（昭和3年）[[九州大学大学院農学研究院・大学院生物資源環境科学府・農学部|九州帝国大学農学部]]農学科を卒業した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
[[高知県庁|高知県]]農林技師、[[宮城県庁|宮城県]]農林技師、[[興亜院]]技師などを務めた{{R|giki}}。戦後[[農林水産省|農林省]]に入省し{{R|shintei}}、農林技師、関東開拓事務所長、農林省東京農地事務局事業部長、同建設部長などを務めた{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
1950年（昭和25年）6月の[[第2回参議院議員通常選挙]]で[[全国区制|全国区]]に[[自由党 (日本 1950-1955)|自由党]]公認で出馬して当選し<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』540頁。</ref>、参議院議員に1期在任した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。1956年（昭和31年）7月の[[第4回参議院議員通常選挙|第4回通常選挙]]で全国区に[[自由民主党 (日本)|自由民主党]]公認で立候補したが落選した<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』547頁。</ref>。
その他、全国土地改良協会理事、農業土木学会（現[[農業農村工学会]]）理事、宮本地質コンサルタント社長などを務めた{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
==脚注== === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *衆議院・参議院『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』大蔵省印刷局、1990年。 *『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』日外アソシエーツ、2003年。 *『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』日外アソシエーツ、2017年。

{{Otheruses|参議院議員|殉職警察官|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}} '''宮本 邦彦'''（みやもと くにひこ、[[1899年]]〈[[明治]]32年〉[[9月6日]]<ref name=shintei>『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』605頁。</ref> - [[1984年]]〈[[昭和]]59年{{R|shintei}}〉[[4月24日]]{{R|shintei}}）は、[[昭和]]期の[[農林水産省|農林]][[技官]]、[[実業家]]、[[政治家]]。[[参議院|参議院議員]]（1期）。
== 経歴 == [[長野県]]<ref name=giki>『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』421頁。</ref>[[長野市]]出身{{R|shintei}}。1928年（昭和3年）[[九州大学大学院農学研究院・大学院生物資源環境科学府・農学部|九州帝国大学農学部]]農学科を卒業した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
[[高知県庁|高知県]]農林技師、[[宮城県庁|宮城県]]農林技師、[[興亜院]]技師などを務めた{{R|giki}}。戦後[[農林水産省|農林省]]に入省し{{R|shintei}}、農林技師、関東開拓事務所長、農林省東京農地事務局事業部長、同建設部長などを務めた{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
1950年（昭和25年）6月の[[第2回参議院議員通常選挙]]で[[全国区制|全国区]]に[[自由党 (日本 1950-1955)|自由党]]公認で出馬して当選し<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』540頁。</ref>、参議院議員に1期在任した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。1956年（昭和31年）7月の[[第4回参議院議員通常選挙|第4回通常選挙]]で全国区に[[自由民主党 (日本)|自由民主党]]公認で立候補したが落選した<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』547頁。</ref>。

1969年（昭和44年）秋の叙勲で[[勲等|勲三等]][[旭日章|旭日中綬章]]受章（勲五等からの昇叙）<ref>{{Cite Kotobank|word=宮本 邦彦|author=新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和|accessdate=2023-02-18}}</ref><ref>『官報』号外第146号2頁 昭和44年11月4日号</ref>。
1984年（昭和59年）4月24日死去、84歳。死没日をもって[[従五位]]から[[従四位]]に叙される<ref>『官報』第17174号14-15頁 昭和59年5月9日号</ref>。

== 参考文献 == *衆議院・参議院『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』大蔵省印刷局、1990年。 *『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』日外アソシエーツ、2003年。 *『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』日外アソシエーツ、2017年。 {{旧全国区選出参議院議員}} {{デフォルトソート:みやもと くにひこ}} [[Category:農林技官]] [[Category:日本の実業家]] [[Category:全国区選出の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由党(日本 1950-1955)の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:昭和時代の参議院議員]] [[Category:従四位受位者]] [[Category:従五位受位者]] [[Category:勲三等旭日中綬章受章者]] [[Category:九州大学出身の人物]] [[Category:長野県出身の人物]] [[Category:1899年生]] [[Category:1984年没]]

revision.comment: カテゴリ
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|参議院議員|殉職警察官|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}} '''宮本 邦彦'''（みやもと くにひこ、[[1899年]]〈[[明治]]32年〉[[9月6日]]<ref name=shintei>『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』605頁。</ref> - [[1984年]]〈[[昭和]]59年{{R|shintei}}〉[[4月24日]]{{R|shintei}}）は、[[昭和]]期の[[農林水産省|農林]][[技官]]、[[実業家]]、[[政治家]]。[[参議院|参議院議員]]（1期）。
== 経歴 == [[長野県]]<ref name=giki>『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』421頁。</ref>[[長野市]]出身{{R|shintei}}。1928年（昭和3年）[[九州大学大学院農学研究院・大学院生物資源環境科学府・農学部|九州帝国大学農学部]]農学科を卒業した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
[[高知県庁|高知県]]農林技師、[[宮城県庁|宮城県]]農林技師、[[興亜院]]技師などを務めた{{R|giki}}。戦後[[農林水産省|農林省]]に入省し{{R|shintei}}、農林技師、関東開拓事務所長、農林省東京農地事務局事業部長、同建設部長などを務めた{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
1950年（昭和25年）6月の[[第2回参議院議員通常選挙]]で[[全国区制|全国区]]に[[自由党 (日本 1950-1955)|自由党]]公認で出馬して当選し<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』540頁。</ref>、参議院議員に1期在任した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。1956年（昭和31年）7月の[[第4回参議院議員通常選挙|第4回通常選挙]]で全国区に[[自由民主党 (日本)|自由民主党]]公認で立候補したが落選した<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』547頁。</ref>。
その他、全国土地改良協会理事、農業土木学会（現[[農業農村工学会]]）理事、宮本地質コンサルタント社長などを務めた{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
1969年（昭和44年）秋の叙勲で[[勲等|勲三等]][[旭日章|旭日中綬章]]受章（勲五等からの昇叙）<ref>{{Cite Kotobank|word=宮本 邦彦|encyclopedia=新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和|accessdate=2023-02-18}}</ref><ref>『官報』号外第146号2頁 昭和44年11月4日号</ref>。
1984年（昭和59年）4月24日死去、84歳。死没日をもって[[従五位]]から[[従四位]]に叙される<ref>『官報』第17174号14-15頁 昭和59年5月9日号</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *衆議院・参議院『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』大蔵省印刷局、1990年。 *『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』日外アソシエーツ、2003年。 *『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』日外アソシエーツ、2017年。 {{旧全国区選出参議院議員}} {{デフォルトソート:みやもと くにひこ}} [[Category:農林技官]] [[Category:日本の実業家]] [[Category:全国区選出の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由党(日本 1950-1955)の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:昭和時代の参議院議員]] [[Category:従四位受位者]] [[Category:従五位受位者]] [[Category:勲三等旭日中綬章受章者]] [[Category:宮城県内の地方公務員出身の人物]] [[Category:九州大学出身の人物]] [[Category:長野県出身の人物]] [[Category:1899年生]] [[Category:1984年没]]

1969年（昭和44年）秋の叙勲で[[勲等|勲三等]][[旭日章|旭日中綬章]]受章（勲五等からの昇叙）<ref>{{Cite Kotobank|word=宮本 邦彦|encyclopedia=新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和|accessdate=2023-02-18}}</ref><ref>『官報』号外第146号2頁 昭和44年11月4日号</ref>。
1984年（昭和59年）4月24日死去、84歳。死没日をもって[[従五位]]から[[従四位]]に叙される<ref>『官報』第17174号14-15頁 昭和59年5月9日号</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *衆議院・参議院『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』大蔵省印刷局、1990年。 *『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』日外アソシエーツ、2003年。 *『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』日外アソシエーツ、2017年。 {{旧全国区選出参議院議員}} {{デフォルトソート:みやもと くにひこ}} [[Category:農林技官]] [[Category:日本の実業家]] [[Category:全国区選出の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由党(日本 1950-1955)の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:昭和時代の参議院議員]] [[Category:従四位受位者]] [[Category:従五位受位者]] [[Category:勲三等旭日中綬章受章者]]

revision.text:
{{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. [[Category:Japanese police officers]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Railway accident deaths]] [[Category:People from Sapporo]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]]

Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. [[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. [[Category:Japanese police officers]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Railway accident deaths]] [[Category:People from Sapporo]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]]

''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. [[Category:Japanese police officers]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Railway accident deaths]] [[Category:People from Sapporo]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kunihiko Miyamoto-qq]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]]

He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese police officers]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Railway accident deaths]] [[Category:People from Sapporo]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]]

ISBN 978-4884714079. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese police officers]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Railway accident deaths]] [[Category:People from Sapporo]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

revision.text:
{{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | Rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese police officers]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Railway accident deaths]] [[Category:People from Sapporo]] [[Category:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: 14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宮本邦彦 up to rev 99458905
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|参議院議員|殉職警察官|宮本邦彦 (警察官)}} '''宮本 邦彦'''（みやもと くにひこ、[[1899年]]〈[[明治]]32年〉[[9月6日]]<ref name=shintei>『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』605頁。</ref> - [[1984年]]〈[[昭和]]59年{{R|shintei}}〉[[4月24日]]{{R|shintei}}）は、[[昭和]]期の[[農林水産省|農林]][[技官]]、[[実業家]]、[[政治家]]。[[参議院|参議院議員]]（1期）。
== 経歴 == [[長野県]]<ref name=giki>『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』421頁。</ref>[[長野市]]出身{{R|shintei}}。1928年（昭和3年）[[九州大学大学院農学研究院・大学院生物資源環境科学府・農学部|九州帝国大学農学部]]農学科を卒業した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
[[高知県庁|高知県]]農林技師、[[宮城県庁|宮城県]]農林技師、[[興亜院]]技師などを務めた{{R|giki}}。戦後[[農林水産省|農林省]]に入省し{{R|shintei}}、農林技師、関東開拓事務所長、農林省東京農地事務局事業部長、同建設部長などを務めた{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
1950年（昭和25年）6月の[[第2回参議院議員通常選挙]]で[[全国区制|全国区]]に[[自由党 (日本 1950-1955)|自由党]]公認で出馬して当選し<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』540頁。</ref>、参議院議員に1期在任した{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。1956年（昭和31年）7月の[[第4回参議院議員通常選挙|第4回通常選挙]]で全国区に[[自由民主党 (日本)|自由民主党]]公認で立候補したが落選した<ref>『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』547頁。</ref>。
その他、全国土地改良協会理事、農業土木学会（現[[農業農村工学会]]）理事、宮本地質コンサルタント社長などを務めた{{R|shintei}}{{R|giki}}。
1969年（昭和44年）秋の叙勲で[[勲等|勲三等]][[旭日章|旭日中綬章]]受章（勲五等からの昇叙）<ref>{{Cite Kotobank|word=宮本 邦彦|encyclopedia=新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和|accessdate=2023-02-18}}</ref><ref>『官報』号外第146号2頁 昭和44年11月4日号</ref>。
1984年（昭和59年）4月24日死去、84歳。死没日をもって[[従五位]]から[[従四位]]に叙される<ref>『官報』第17174号14-15頁 昭和59年5月9日号</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *衆議院・参議院『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』大蔵省印刷局、1990年。 *『新訂 政治家人名事典 明治～昭和』日外アソシエーツ、2003年。 *『国政選挙総覧 1947-2016』日外アソシエーツ、2017年。 {{旧全国区選出参議院議員}} {{デフォルトソート:みやもと くにひこ}} [[Category:農林技官]] [[Category:日本の実業家]] [[Category:全国区選出の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由党(日本 1950-1955)の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:昭和時代の参議院議員]] [[Category:従四位受位者]] [[Category:従五位受位者]] [[Category:勲三等旭日中綬章受章者]]
[[Category:九州大学出身の人物]] [[Category:長野県出身の人物]] [[Category:1899年生]] [[Category:1984年没]]

== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}

In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | Rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]]

{{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | Rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:全国区選出の参議院議員]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:従五位受位者]] [[Category:従四位受位者]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]]

{{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | Rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[February 17]], [[1953]] – [[February 12]], [[2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[February 17]], [[1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:従五位受位者]] [[Category:従四位受位者]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:昭和時代の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由党(日本 1950-1955)の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:農林技官]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]]

He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[February 6]], [[2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.
