[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]] – [[:en:February 12|February 12]], [[:en:2007|2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Itabashi|Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | Rank = [[Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]] – [[:en:February 12|February 12]], [[:en:2007|2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[:en:inspector|inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[:en:Hokkai Gakuen University|Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:従五位受位者]] [[Category:従四位受位者]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:昭和時代の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由党(日本 1950-1955)の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:農林技官]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]]

[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]] – [[:en:February 12|February 12]], [[:en:2007|2007]]) was a Japanese [[police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[:en:inspector|inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]], in [[Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[:en:Hokkai Gakuen University|Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[:en:June 18|June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:昭和時代の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由党(日本 1950-1955)の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:農林技官]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]]

''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Itabashi|Itabashi]], [[Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[:en:Police officer|Police officer]] | employer = [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | Rank = [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]] – [[:en:February 12|February 12]], [[:en:2007|2007]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Police officer|Police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[:en:inspector|inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]], in [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[:en:Hokkai Gakuen University|Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[:en:June 18|June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:昭和時代の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由党(日本 1950-1955)の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:Agricultural and Forestry Technical Officers]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]]

''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{Birth date|1953|02|17}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | death_date = {{Death date and age|1953|02|17|2007|02|12}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Itabashi|Itabashi]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | nationality = Japanese | occupation = [[:en:Police officer|Police officer]] | employer = [[:en:Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | Rank = [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]] – [[:en:February 12|February 12]], [[:en:2007|2007]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Police officer|Police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[:en:Tobu Tojo Line|Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[:en:sergeant|sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[:en:inspector|inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]], in [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[:en:Hokkai Gakuen University|Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[:en:Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[:en:June 18|June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:昭和時代の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由党(日本 1950-1955)の参議院議員]] [[Category:自由民主党の参議院議員]] [[Category:Agricultural and Forestry Technical Officers]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]]

''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97172113]] Wikidata name: Kunihiko Miyamoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{birth date|1899|9|6}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | death_date = {{death date|1984|4|24}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Itabashi|Itabashi]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | nationality = {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}} | occupation = {{ill|politician|qid=Q82955}} | employer = [[:en:Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | Rank = [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] | gender= {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|member of the House of Councillors|qid=Q14552828}} }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]] – [[:en:February 12|February 12]], [[:en:2007|2007]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Police officer|Police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[:en:Tobu Tojo Line|Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[:en:sergeant|sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[:en:inspector|inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]], in [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[:en:Hokkai Gakuen University|Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[:en:Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[:en:June 18|June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kunihiko Miyamoto]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors in the Shōwa Era]] [[Category:Members of the Japan Liberal Party Senate 1950-1955]] [[Category:Members of the Liberal Democratic Party Senate]] [[Category:Agricultural and Forestry Technical Officers]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]]

[[Senior Seventh Rank]] | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|member of the House of Councillors|WD=Q14552828}} }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]] – [[:en:February 12|February 12]], [[:en:2007|2007]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Police officer|Police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[:en:Tobu Tojo Line|Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[:en:sergeant|sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[:en:inspector|inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]], in [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[:en:Hokkai Gakuen University|Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[:en:Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[:en:June 18|June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors in the Shōwa Era]] [[Category:Members of the Japan Liberal Party Senate 1950-1955]] [[Category:Members of the Liberal Democratic Party Senate]] [[Category:Agricultural and Forestry Technical Officers]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q97172113}}

In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[:en:June 18|June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[:en:sergeant|sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[:en:inspector|inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]], in [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[:en:Hokkai Gakuen University|Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[:en:Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[:en:June 18|June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[ja:宮本邦彦]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97172113]] Wikidata name: Kunihiko Miyamoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{birth date|1899|9|6}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | death_date = {{death date|1984|4|24}} | death_place = {{JPN}} [[:en:Itabashi|Itabashi]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | nationality = {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} | occupation = {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}} | employer = [[:en:Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]] | Rank = [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|member of the House of Councillors|WD=Q14552828}} }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]] – [[:en:February 12|February 12]], [[:en:2007|2007]]) was a Japanese [[:en:Police officer|Police officer]] who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[:en:Tobu Tojo Line|Tobu Tojo Line]]'s [[:en:Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|Tokiwadai Station]] crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher=[[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]]|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a [[:en:sergeant|sergeant]] at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to [[:en:inspector|inspector]]{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on [[:en:February 17|February 17]], [[:en:1953|1953]], in [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[:en:Hokkai Gakuen University|Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[:en:Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[:en:June 18|June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:宮本邦彦]] [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors in the Shōwa Era]] [[Category:Members of the Japan Liberal Party Senate 1950-1955]] [[Category:Members of the Liberal Democratic Party Senate]] [[Category:Agricultural and Forestry Technical Officers]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q97172113}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from [[:en:Hokkai Gakuen University|Hokkai Gakuen University]] in [[:en:1976|1976]], Miyamoto joined the [[:en:Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department]]. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in [[:en:1976|1976]]. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In [[:en:2004|2004]], he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of [[:en:February 6|February 6]], [[:en:2007|2007]], Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the [[:en:Order of the Rising Sun|Order of the Rising Sun]], Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on [[:en:June 18|June 18]], [[:en:2007|2007]]. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:宮本邦彦]] {{wikidata link|Q97172113}} [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Agricultural and Forestry Technical Officers]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors in the Shōwa Era]] [[Category:Members of the Japan Liberal Party Senate 1950-1955]] [[Category:Members of the Liberal Democratic Party Senate]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from {{ill|Hokkai Gakuen University|en|Hokkai Gakuen University|WD=Q4120696|lt=Hokkai Gakuen University}} in {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, Miyamoto joined the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|en|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|WD=Q1312559|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department}}. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}}, he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of {{ill|February 6|en|February 6|WD=Q2320|lt=February 6}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}}, Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the {{ill|Order of the Rising Sun|en|Order of the Rising Sun|WD=Q818600|lt=Order of the Rising Sun}}, Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|WD=Q2670|lt=June 18}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}}. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:宮本邦彦]] {{wikidata link|Q97172113}} [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Agricultural and Forestry Technical Officers]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors in the Shōwa Era]] [[Category:Members of the Japan Liberal Party Senate 1950-1955]] [[Category:Members of the Liberal Democratic Party Senate]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[Senior Seventh Rank]] | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|member of the House of Councillors|WD=Q14552828}} }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, {{ill|February 17|en|February 17|WD=Q2341|lt=February 17}}, {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} – {{ill|February 12|en|February 12|WD=Q2336|lt=February 12}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}}) was a Japanese {{ill|Police officer|en|Police officer|WD=Q384593|lt=Police officer}} who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[:en:Tobu Tojo Line|Tobu Tojo Line]]'s {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|en|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q7813626|lt=Tokiwadai Station}} crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher={{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=The Asahi Shimbun}}|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a {{ill|sergeant|en|sergeant|WD=Q157696|lt=sergeant}} at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to {{ill|inspector|en|inspector|WD=Q511138|lt=inspector}}{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun}}}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on {{ill|February 17|en|February 17|WD=Q2341|lt=February 17}}, {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}}, in {{ill|Sapporo|en|Sapporo|WD=Q37951|lt=Sapporo}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from {{ill|Hokkai Gakuen University|en|Hokkai Gakuen University|WD=Q4120696|lt=Hokkai Gakuen University}} in {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, Miyamoto joined the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|en|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|WD=Q1312559|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department}}. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}}, he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of {{ill|February 6|en|February 6|WD=Q2320|lt=February 6}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}}, Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the {{ill|Order of the Rising Sun|en|Order of the Rising Sun|WD=Q818600|lt=Order of the Rising Sun}}, Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|WD=Q2670|lt=June 18}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}}. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. At the time of his death, he was serving as a {{ill|sergeant|en|sergeant|WD=Q157696|lt=sergeant|1=Sergeant}} at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to {{ill|inspector|en|inspector|WD=Q511138|lt=inspector|1=Inspector}}{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|WD=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on {{ill|February 17|en|February 17|WD=Q2341|lt=February 17|1=February 17}}, {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}}, in {{ill|Sapporo|en|Sapporo|WD=Q37951|lt=Sapporo|1=Sapporo}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from {{ill|Hokkai Gakuen University|en|Hokkai Gakuen University|WD=Q4120696|lt=Hokkai Gakuen University|1=Hokkai Gakuen University}} in {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}, Miyamoto joined the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|en|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|WD=Q1312559|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department}}. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}, he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of {{ill|February 6|en|February 6|WD=Q2320|lt=February 6|1=February 6}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}, Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the {{ill|Order of the Rising Sun|en|Order of the Rising Sun|WD=Q818600|lt=Order of the Rising Sun|1=Order of the Rising Sun}}, Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|WD=Q2670|lt=June 18|1=June 18}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:宮本邦彦]] {{wikidata link|Q97172113}} [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Agricultural and Forestry Technical Officers]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors in the Shōwa Era]] [[Category:Members of the Japan Liberal Party Senate 1950-1955]] [[Category:Members of the Liberal Democratic Party Senate]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from {{ill|Hokkai Gakuen University|en|Hokkai Gakuen University|qid=Q4120696|lt=Hokkai Gakuen University|1=Hokkai Gakuen University}} in {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}, Miyamoto joined the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|en|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|qid=Q1312559|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department}}. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In {{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}, he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of {{ill|February 6|en|February 6|qid=Q2320|lt=February 6|1=February 6}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}, Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the {{ill|Order of the Rising Sun|en|Order of the Rising Sun|qid=Q818600|lt=Order of the Rising Sun|1=Order of the Rising Sun}}, Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|qid=Q2670|lt=June 18|1=June 18}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008. ISBN 978-4884714079. {{translated page|ja|宮本邦彦|version=99458905|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:宮本邦彦]] {{wikidata link|Q97172113}} [[Category:1899 births]] [[Category:1953 births]] [[Category:1984 deaths]] [[Category:2007 deaths]] [[Category:Agricultural and Forestry Technical Officers]] [[Category:Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Kyushu University alumni]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors from the National District]] [[Category:Members of the House of Councillors in the Shōwa Era]] [[Category:Members of the Japan Liberal Party Senate 1950-1955]] [[Category:Members of the Liberal Democratic Party Senate]] [[Category:People from Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 3rd class]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{How to reference|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunihiko Miyamoto | native_name = 宮本 邦彦 | native_name_lang= ja | image = 東武東上線ときわ台駅前宮本邦彦警部補顕彰「誠の碑」.JPG | caption = Memorial for Kunihiko Miyamoto, "Makoto no Hi" | birth_date = {{birth date|1899|9|6}} | birth_place = {{JPN}} {{ill|Sapporo|en|Sapporo|qid=Q37951|lt=Sapporo|1=Sapporo}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | death_date = {{death date|1984|4|24}} | death_place = {{JPN}} {{ill|Itabashi|en|Itabashi|qid=Q232635|lt=Itabashi|1=Itabashi}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}} | nationality = {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} | occupation = {{ill|politician|qid=Q82955|1=Politician}} | employer = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|en|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|qid=Q1312559|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department}} | Rank = [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]] (posthumous promotion) | awards = {{ill|Order of the Rising Sun|en|Order of the Rising Sun|qid=Q818600|lt=Order of the Rising Sun|1=Order of the Rising Sun}}, Gold and Silver Rays
[[Senior Seventh Rank]] | gender= {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | title= {{ill|member of the House of Councillors|qid=Q14552828|1=member of the House of Councillors}} }} '''Kunihiko Miyamoto''' (宮本 邦彦, {{ill|February 17|en|February 17|qid=Q2341|lt=February 17|1=February 17}}, {{ill|1953|en|1953|qid=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} – {{ill|February 12|en|February 12|qid=Q2336|lt=February 12|1=February 12}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}) was a Japanese {{ill|Police officer|en|Police officer|qid=Q384593|lt=Police officer|1=Police officer}} who died while attempting to rescue a woman at the [[:en:Tobu Tojo Line|Tobu Tojo Line]]'s {{ill|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|en|Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)|qid=Q7813626|lt=Tokiwadai Station|1=Tokiwadai Station (Tokyo)}} crossing{{Cite news|title=Police officer dies after attempting to rescue a woman in Tokyo's Itabashi Ward|newspaper=Asahi.com|date=February 12, 2007|url=http://www.asahi.com/national/update/0212/TKY200702120142.html|publisher={{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|qid=Q720503|lt=The Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}|language=ja}}. At the time of his death, he was serving as a {{ill|sergeant|en|sergeant|qid=Q157696|lt=sergeant|1=Sergeant}} at the [[Itabashi Police Station]] and posthumously promoted two Ranks to {{ill|inspector|en|inspector|qid=Q511138|lt=inspector|1=Inspector}}{{Cite news|title=Officer Miyamoto honored 10 years after his heroic death at Tokiwadai Station crossing|newspaper=Sankei News|date=February 5, 2017|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20170205-D6C4KTRJO5L25D6JDJTTLQTMNA/|publisher={{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|qid=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun|1=Sankei Shimbun}}}}. == Early Life == Kunihiko Miyamoto was born on {{ill|February 17|en|February 17|qid=Q2341|lt=February 17|1=February 17}}, {{ill|1953|en|1953|qid=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}}, in {{ill|Sapporo|en|Sapporo|qid=Q37951|lt=Sapporo|1=Sapporo}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. He reportedly struggled with physical activities as a child but showed resilience in overcoming challenges. He started delivering newspapers as a middle school student and adopted the phrase "伏してぞ止まん" (I will not stop, even if I must bow) as his personal mottoYamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.. == Career == After graduating from {{ill|Hokkai Gakuen University|en|Hokkai Gakuen University|qid=Q4120696|lt=Hokkai Gakuen University|1=Hokkai Gakuen University}} in {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}, Miyamoto joined the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|en|Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|qid=Q1312559|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department}}. While at the police academy, he focused on improving his kendo skills, eventually achieving the Rank of third dan. Miyamoto’s first assignment was in the patrol division of the [[Ōmori Police Station]] in {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}. Over the years, he served in several positions, including at the [[Machida Police Station]] and [[Kitasenju Police Station]]. In {{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}, he was assigned to the [[Itabashi Police Station]], working at the Tokiwadai Police Box near the site of his eventual death. == Death in the Line of Duty == On the evening of {{ill|February 6|en|February 6|qid=Q2320|lt=February 6|1=February 6}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}, Miyamoto received a report of a woman trespassing on the railway tracks near Tokiwadai Station. After initially guiding her to safety, she ran back onto the tracks. Miyamoto attempted to rescue her as a train approached. While he successfully pushed her into a safe position, Miyamoto was struck by the train. He succumbed to his injuries six days later. == Posthumous Recognition == Following his death, Miyamoto was: * Promoted posthumously to [[:en:Police inspector|Police inspector]]. * Awarded the [[Senior Seventh Rank]] and the {{ill|Order of the Rising Sun|en|Order of the Rising Sun|qid=Q818600|lt=Order of the Rising Sun|1=Order of the Rising Sun}}, Gold and Silver Rays, by [[:en:Prime Minister Shinzo Abe|Prime Minister Shinzo Abe]]''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 2, 2007.. * Honored with a memorial, "Makoto no Hi," near Tokiwadai Station on {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|qid=Q2670|lt=June 18|1=June 18}}, {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}. * The subject of a picture book and a television drama titled ''[[Don't Die! Real-Life Drama: What Inspector Miyamoto Left Behind]]'' ([[:en:Fuji TV|Fuji TV]], 2008). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamaguchi, Hidenori. ''Fushite zo Toman: Boku, Miyamoto Keibu desu.'' Takagi Shobo, 2008.
